title: Notopleura lateriflora
taxonomy: Notopleura lateriflora is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This classification is based on its characteristic features and phylogenetic relationships with other species within the family. The genus Notopleura is a part of the tribe Psychotrieae, which includes various species of shrubs and trees found in tropical regions. The tribe Psychotrieae is a subfamily of Rubiaceae known for its unique floral structures and adaptations. Notopleura lateriflora is a specific species within this complex taxonomic classification.

intro: Notopleura lateriflora, also known as Pilocarpus notopleurus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical rainforests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. Bioactive constituents of Notopleura lateriflora include alkaloids, such as notopleurine and pilocarpine, as well as flavonoids and other phenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
